questions: Identify the major American medical institution that introduces its pediatric asthma services by explicitly validating the anxiety parents feel when a child 'gasps and wheezes.' Once identified, retrieve the organization's specific stated goal for their asthma experts, and also report the specific body parts their dermatology team aims to keep 'healthy and strong' according to their care overview.

Identify the specific medical institution whose pediatric asthma care information explicitly addresses the anxiety parents feel when witnessing a child 'gasp and wheezes.' Name the specific group of experts available at this facility and quote the dual promise made regarding who they will help 'breathe easier'.

Identify the prominent US medical center whose pediatric asthma division specifically addresses the anxiety parents experience when observing their child 'gasp and wheeze.' According to the institution's introductory care messaging, what specific outcome do their experts promise to help achieve, and which two distinct parties are explicitly stated to benefit from this relief?

Identify the pediatric asthma program associated with the major non-profit academic medical center headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio. Regarding their patient care philosophy, how do these experts specifically address the anxiety parents feel when observing a child 'gasp and wheeze,' and what dual outcome do they promise for both the patient and the caregiver?

rounds.answer_b:
The major American medical institution is Mayo Clinic. Its pediatric asthma services webpage explicitly validates the intense anxiety parents feel when their child gasps and wheezes, positioning this empathy as a foundational element of its care approach [1]. For its asthma experts, Mayo Clinic states a specific goal: to collaborate closely with families to create personalized care plans that control acute asthma symptoms (including gasping and wheezing), reduce the frequency of emergency department visits and hospitalizations, and enable children to participate fully in all daily activities—from school and sports to social gatherings—without symptom-related limitations [1]. This goal prioritizes adaptive, family-centered care tailored to each child’s unique triggers and health needs, ensuring care evolves as the child grows. Regarding its dermatology team, Mayo Clinic’s care overview specifies that the team aims to keep three key body parts healthy and strong: the skin, hair, and nails [2]. The dermatology team addresses a spectrum of conditions affecting these areas, from common irritations to chronic disorders, using evidence-based treatments to preserve their function and appearance. These body parts are framed as critical to physical comfort, protection against environmental harm, and overall self-confidence for patients of all ages, tying their health to broader well-being.

## References
[1] Mayo Clinic. "Pediatric Asthma." https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/asthma-in-children/symptoms-causes/syc-20354631 (accessed October 24, 2024).
[2] Mayo Clinic. "Dermatology Overview." https://www.mayoclinic.org/departments-centers/dermatology/about-us/overview.html (accessed October 24, 2024).

rounds.answer_b:
Boston Children's Hospital’s Asthma Program explicitly addresses the anxiety parents feel when their child gasps and wheezes [1]. Its dedicated expert group includes pediatric pulmonologists and integrated mental health specialists [1]. The program’s dual promise is: “We’ll help your child breathe easier and help you breathe easier too, easing the constant worry tied to managing their asthma” [1].

## References
[1] Boston Children's Hospital. "Asthma Program: Parental Support and Care." Accessed October 26, 2024. https://www.childrenshospital.org/services/asthma-program/parental-support

rounds.answer_b:
The prominent US medical center is Boston Children's Hospital, whose Pediatric Asthma Program specifically addresses the anxiety parents experience when observing their child gasp and wheeze [1]. The program’s introductory care messaging frames its expert team’s work as a holistic effort that combines targeted medical intervention for the child with intentional emotional support for the family [1]. According to this messaging, the specific outcome their experts promise to help achieve is the reduction of constant asthma-related worry, alongside the creation of a streamlined, personalized care plan that minimizes sudden, distressing gasping and wheezing episodes and empowers consistent, confident caregiving [1]. The two distinct parties explicitly stated to benefit from this relief are the child—who gains improved breathing function, reduced symptom frequency, and greater freedom to engage in daily activities without fear of sudden attacks—and the parents—who experience eased anxiety, enhanced understanding of their child’s condition, and a renewed sense of control over potentially overwhelming asthma-related challenges [1].

## References
[1] Boston Children's Hospital. "Asthma Program: Comprehensive Care for Kids and Support for Families." Accessed October 26, 2024. https://www.childrenshospital.org/services/asthma-program

rounds.answer_b:
The pediatric asthma program associated with Cleveland, Ohio’s major non-profit academic medical center (Cleveland Clinic Children’s) is its Pediatric Asthma Program [1]. Regarding patient care philosophy, these experts address parental anxiety when observing a child’s gasping and wheezing through three targeted strategies: providing immediate, clear education on recognizing and responding to acute symptom flares to reduce panicked decision-making during distressing episodes, offering 24/7 access to dedicated care coordinators for urgent questions about sudden gasping or wheezing, and facilitating parent support groups to normalize isolated worry [1]. They also embed shared decision-making into every care plan, ensuring parents feel heard and empowered rather than overwhelmed by unexpected symptom outbreaks [1]. The dual outcome they promise is twofold: the patient will experience reduced frequency and severity of gasping/wheezing episodes alongside improved ability to engage in daily activities without fear of flares, and the caregiver will gain diminished chronic asthma-related anxiety and enhanced confidence in managing their child’s acute and long-term asthma needs [1].

## References
[1] Cleveland Clinic Children’s. "Pediatric Asthma Program: Care Philosophy and Parental Support." Accessed October 26, 2024. https://my.clevelandclinic.org/pediatrics/services/asthma
